title: cyperus digitatus var. khasiana
intro: Cyperus digitatus var. khasiana, also known as Khasi Finger Grass, is a variant of the Cyperus digitatus plant, a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, attributed to its medicinal properties such as antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Its health benefits include aiding in wound healing, reducing fever, and providing relief from respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In traditional medicine, it is often used in herbal preparations like decoctions, infusions, and powders to treat various ailments.
